description: Receive and process incoming webhooks from external services with signature verification (HMAC/RSA), replay protection, idempotent deduplication, and async handler routing

fields.name: source
fields.label: Webhook source identifier (e.g., provider name or integration ID)

fields.name: event_type
fields.label: Event type from the external service (e.g., payment.completed)

fields.name: signature
fields.label: Cryptographic signature from the webhook header

fields.name: timestamp
fields.label: Webhook timestamp from the provider header

fields.name: event_id
fields.label: Unique event identifier for idempotency deduplication

fields.name: handler_id
fields.label: Registered handler that processes this event type

fields.name: webhook_secret
fields.label: Shared secret or public key for signature verification

signature_verification: Every incoming webhook must include a cryptographic signature in the request header
HMAC-SHA256: compute HMAC of raw body using shared secret; compare with provided signature
RSA-SHA256: verify signature using provider's public key against raw body
Reject webhook immediately if signature does not match (do not process payload)
Use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks on signature verification
replay_protection: Reject webhooks with timestamp older than 5 minutes from current server time
Clock skew tolerance of 30 seconds to account for network delays
Store processed event_ids for deduplication window (minimum 24 hours)
idempotency: Deduplicate by event_id; if event_id already processed, return 200 without re-processing
Deduplication window is 24 hours; events older than 24 hours may be reprocessed
processing: Store raw payload before processing (audit trail and replay capability)
Route events to registered handlers based on source + event_type combination
Process handlers asynchronously; return 200 immediately to webhook sender
Failed handlers retry 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 4s, 16s)
response: Always return 200 status to webhook sender after signature verification
Return 401 for invalid signatures; return 400 for malformed payloads
Provider will retry on non-2xx responses; avoid unnecessary retries by responding quickly

given: Signature verification passes against raw payload
webhook_received_and_verified.result: Webhook stored and verified; 200 returned to sender; async processing initiated

given: Handler registered for source + event_type combination
webhook_processed_successfully.result: Webhook event processed by registered handler; status marked completed

given: Handler throws an error during processing
webhook_handler_failed_with_retry.result: Handler retried with exponential backoff; retry count incremented

webhook_handler_failed_permanently.result: Handler exhausted all retries; webhook marked as failed for manual investigation

given: Computed signature does not match provided signature
webhook_signature_invalid.result: 401 returned; webhook rejected without storing or processing payload

given: Timestamp is older than 5 minutes from current server time
webhook_replay_detected.result: 400 returned; stale webhook rejected as potential replay attack

given: event_id already exists in processed events store
webhook_duplicate_event.result: 200 returned without re-processing; duplicate event silently ignored

given: No handler registered for the source + event_type combination
webhook_no_handler.result: Webhook stored for audit but no processing performed; 200 returned to sender

errors.message: Webhook signature verification failed. Payload may have been tampered with.
Webhook timestamp is too old. Possible replay attack detected.
Webhook handler failed after all retry attempts. Manual investigation required.
No handler registered for this event type. Webhook stored but not processed.
Webhook payload could not be parsed. Expected valid JSON body.

related.reason: Receive payment status updates via provider webhooks
Receive email delivery status notifications via webhooks
Queue verified webhooks for async handler processing
Route incoming webhooks through gateway for rate limiting
